title: eriogonum umbellatum var. covillei
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. covillei, also known as the sulfurey buckwheat, is a variant of the Sulphur Buckwheat plant, a flowering perennial native to North America. The medicinal properties of Eriogonum umbellatum var. covillei include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Eriogonum umbellatum var. covillei can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional ceremonies by some Native American tribes.
